source_intake_record.raw_question_text: An infinitely long straight conductor is bent into a circle of radius \( R \) at point \( P \). When a current \( I \) flows through it, the magnitude of the magnetic flux density at the center \( O \) of the circle is:
extraction_notes: solution field contains only option letter; mapped to corresponding choice text
image field is a PIL image object placeholder, not a path/URL

image_dependency: The question refers to geometric entities and locations ('bent into a circle', point P, center O), which typically rely on a diagram for exact setup interpretation
multi_step: The target quantity is the magnetic field at the center of a composite conductor geometry, which likely requires combining contributions from distinct parts of the conductor
The answer and choices are nontrivial symbolic expressions involving both constant and 1/pi terms, consistent with a multi-part derivation
This is a physics derivation-style multiple-choice item rather than a direct fact recall question
verifiability: A concrete closed-form symbolic answer is provided and can be checked against the listed choices

risk_flags: diagram-dependent geometry may be underspecified in text alone

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, so the core semantics are recoverable
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
high image dependency is acceptable here because the required diagram asset exists
verifiability is strong due to a concrete short-text answer and high verifiability score
